summary: Soaring Keep On Growing, fiery Lost Sailor > Saint, standout Space jam
blurb: The February ’86 Kaiser show delivers a soaring "Keep On Growing" and a fiery "Lost Sailor > Saint of Circumstance" that lift the second set. Brent’s funky keys and an unforgettable drum‑/Space segment add extra sparkle. Overall the performance is strong with only a few rough spots.
review: On February 14, 1986 at Oakland’s Henry J. Kaiser Convention Center the Dead were in top form. The first set rolls smoothly from "Feel Like A Stranger" into a bright "Let It Grow," but it’s the second set that really shines – Jerry rides a soaring lead on "Keep On Growing" before launching into a blistering "Lost Sailor > Saint of Circumstance" that many reviewers called “fiery.”  The band pushes forward through a powerhouse "Terrapin Station," then hands the reins to Mickey and Bill for what several fans describe as the best drums/Space ever heard, with Brent’s funky keyboards weaving through the improvisation.  Highlights round out the night with a heartfelt "Wharf Rat," a punchy "Throwing Stones" and a soulful encore of "Brokedown Palace."  While a few reviewers note minor slip‑ups in lyric delivery on lesser‑known cuts, the overall momentum stays high, making this show a must‑listen for anyone chasing that mid‑80s magic.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Four recordings are clearly positive, praising standout songs (Keep On Growing, Lost Sailor > Saint, Space) and overall energy; one mixed review notes slight eclipsing but does not detract from the strong consensus. The consistency of praise across multiple sources warrants a solid positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Crystal‑clear Charlie Miller SBD with excellent instrument separation; captures the soaring "Keep On Growing" and fiery Lost Sailor > Saint sequence without dropouts.

key_highlights: Soaring "Keep On Growing" that lifts the second set.
"Lost Sailor > Saint of Circumstance" praised as a fiery, high‑energy pairing.
Exceptional drums/Space segment hailed as one of the best ever.
Brent’s funky keyboard work adds groove throughout.

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia delivers rocking, funky leads and soaring solos on "Keep On Growing" and the Lost Sailor > Saint sequence, keeping the energy high despite a minor lyric slip.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh provides a steady, grounding bass foundation and conducts the band with his characteristic waving style, holding everything together even amid health rumors.
band_performance.Brent: Mydland’s keyboard playing is notably funky, adding sparkle to "Let It Grow" and driving the improvisational sections with rhythmic flair.
band_performance.Mickey: Hart’s percussion shines in the drum/Space segment, delivering what many call the best drums ever heard on this night.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann locks in tight, propulsive grooves that complement Mickey’s explorations and keep the momentum rolling through the second set.
